meanings.definition: A person who assists or follows a religious leader during ceremonies or rituals.
examples: The young acolyte carried candles during the ceremony.
Several acolytes stood beside the priest in the temple.
The acolytes prepared the altar before the service began.
An elderly monk trained new acolytes in religious duties.

meanings.definition: A loyal follower, supporter, or admirer of an important person, leader, or idea.
examples: The famous writer was surrounded by enthusiastic acolytes.
Political acolytes defended the leader at every opportunity.
The scientist’s acolytes continued his research after his death.
Young musicians became acolytes of the legendary performer.

meanings.definition: Painful conditions, sufferings, illnesses, or troubles that cause physical or emotional distress.
examples: The village suffered from many afflictions during the drought.
Old age often brings physical afflictions.
She endured personal afflictions with great courage.
The hospital treated patients with rare afflictions.

meanings.definition: Mental, emotional, or spiritual suffering that deeply troubles a person.
examples: Loneliness became one of his greatest afflictions.
The novel explores the hidden afflictions of modern life.
Fear and guilt were afflicting his mind constantly.
Many poets write about emotional afflictions and sorrow.
